item.title: US billionaire owners of Crystal Palace explore sale
item.description: South London football club is enjoying its most successful ever period

item.title: ConocoPhillips set to sign deal with Syria to revive gas production
item.description: Agreement will be first by a US energy major with Damascus as it aims to rebuild activity after years of civil war

item.title: Murdochs’ aversion to big buyouts meets its match in the streaming age
item.description: Fox’s shares dip following news of $22bn acquisition of Roku

item.title: AI giants are learning a hard lesson about pricing power
item.description: Anthropic, until Friday’s White House move, looked like one of the more rationally valued companies in its peer group

item.title: Stocks surge as US-Iran deal ignites global rally
item.description: Investor sentiment also helped by SpaceX’s historic initial public offering

item.title: Rolls-Royce wins contract to build small nuclear reactors for Sweden
item.description: UK firm’s SMR unit beats competition from GE Vernova Hitachi for its third major contract

item.title: Cutting access to Anthropic’s Mythos is a gift to China
item.description: Washington’s suspension just strengthened Beijing’s pitch for its own competing AI models

item.title: UK financial regulator gears up to levy bigger fines
item.description: Move by FCA comes after it was forced to reduce penalties by recent court challenges

item.title: RSM expands transatlantic alliance in battle with private equity-backed rivals
item.description: Mexico joins US firm’s partnership with UK, Ireland and Canada to win more clients

item.title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $20bn in first bond deal since 2021
item.description: Debt sale set to test investor appetite for further exposure to AI sector amid a deluge of borrowing

item.title: SpaceX shares gain for second day after blockbuster debut
item.description: Stock in Elon Musk’s AI, satellite and rocket group climbs 8% in early trading on Monday

item.title: How UniCredit won support for its lowball Commerzbank bid
item.description: Tender offer tactics used by investment banker Andrea Orcel deliver latest twist in 21-month takeover battle

item.title: Fox to acquire streaming platform Roku for $22bn
item.description: Combined company will become third-largest in US television by share of viewing as media landscape shifts dramatically

item.title: Me and my exoskeletons: the rise of wearable robotics
item.description: Lighter and more affordable devices give users a battery-powered spring in their step

item.title: How will the UK’s social media ban work?
item.description: Keir Starmer’s government has pledged to go further than Australia despite technical challenges

item.title: Arson targeting Keir Starmer properties originated in Russia
item.description: Handler was affiliated with a pro-Kremlin hacktivist group

item.title: France in talks with UAE as defence plans with Germany fall apart
item.description: Paris’s negotiations with Gulf come after Berlin pulled plug on joint fighter jet and amid delayed tank project

item.title: China slams Indonesia investment climate over nickel curbs
item.description: Regulatory changes could threaten $50bn of investment, embassy warns

item.title: Investor support for executive pay rises even as awards rocket
item.description: Shareholders tend to vote for high compensation as long as it is linked to performance

item.title: Losers in ‘creditor-on-creditor violence’ left with crumbs
item.description: Moody’s analysis of adversarial corporate debt exchanges shows losers recover just 14 cents on the dollar

item.title: Elliott presses Bunzl for buybacks after building stake
item.description: Business supplies distributor is latest in string of UK targets for US activist hedge fund

item.title: Social media ban reveals Starmer’s coherence problem
item.description: If 16-year-olds are old enough to vote, they should have access to the same information environment as adults

item.title: Ban on TikTok and YouTube will make children ‘happier’, says Starmer
item.description: PM announces that he expects ban on under-16s using some platforms to be in place early next year

item.title: The Iran war in seven charts
item.description: From oil markets to reconstruction, the fallout from the conflict will unfold over years

item.title: Starbucks Korea to close stores for staff training after ‘Tank Day’ scandal
item.description: Coffee chain’s owner says move ‘shows how seriously we are taking the marketing incident’

item.title: China summons Walmart chain Sam’s Club over food safety issues
item.description: US retailer has expanded rapidly in world’s second-largest economy

item.title: The spectacle of SpaceX trumps DCF models
item.description: The public focused on the mission, not the multiple

item.title: Sweden demands other EU states tackle Russian oil tankers in their waters
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Kallas fronts up amid EEAS reform talk

item.title: How SpaceX hit its $2tn valuation
item.description: Plus, end of an era for Asia’s slickest brokerage, the US stock supply drought is over, and some striking Kapoor in London

item.title: FirstFT: Iran and US agree Hormuz deal
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Ukraine’s drone campaign and BBC job cuts
